title: satureja acinos var. canescens
intro: Satureja acinos var. canescens, also known as woodland savory, is a perennial herb belonging to the Lamiaceae family. It is a variant of the Satureja acinos plant, characterized by its hairy stems and leaves, typically found in woodland areas of Europe. Woodland savory has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Satureja acinos var. canescens include relief from digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin irritations, and it can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or essential oil.
